`view-forecast-button`: Button, navigates to Weekly Forecast page\n  - `view-alerts-button`: Button, navigates to Weather Alerts page\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - `search-location-button` → `url_for('location_search')`\n  - `view-forecast-button` → `url_for('weekly_forecast')`\n  - `view-alerts-button` → `url_for('weather_alerts')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `default_location` (dict) with `location_id`, `location_name`, `temperature`, `condition`\n  - `saved_locations` (list of dicts) with `location_id`, `location_name`\n- Usage notes:\n  - Display current weather summary for `default_location` in `current-weather-summary`\n  - Provide quick navigation buttons to other pages\n\n### 2. templates/current_weather.html\n- Page Title: \"Current Weather\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `location_name` (str)\n  - `temperature` (float/int)\n  - `condition` (str)\n  - `humidity` (int)\n  - `wind_speed` (float/int)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Display detailed weather info for selected location\n\n### 3. templates/weekly_forecast.html\n- Page Title: \"Weekly Forecast\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `forecast-page`: Div, main container\n  - `forecast-table`: Table, displays daily forecasts (date, high temp, low temp, condition)\n  - `location-filter`: Dropdown, filter forecasts by location\n  - `forecast-list`: Div, grid display of forecast cards for each day\n  - `back-to-dashboard`: Button, navigates back to dashboard\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - `back-to-dashboard` → `url_for('dashboard')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)\n  - `selected_location_id` (int)\n  - `forecasts` (list of dicts with date, high_temp, low_temp, condition)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Use dropdown `location-filter` to select location and reload forecasts\n  - Loop through `forecasts` to populate `forecast-list` and `forecast-table`\n\n### 4. templates/location_search.html\n- Page Title: \"Search Locations\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `search-page`: Div, main container\n  - `location-search-input`: Input field for search query\n  - `search-results`: Div, displays list of matching locations\n  - `select-location-button-{location_id}`: Button for each search result to select location\n  - `saved-locations-list`: Div, displays previously saved locations\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - Buttons `select-location-button-{location_id}` trigger backend POST to select location\n- Context variables:\n  - `search_results` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name, latitude, longitude)\n  - `saved_locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Dynamic generation of `select-location-button-{location_id}` for each search result\n  - Display saved locations with quick access\n\n### 5. templates/alerts.html\n- Page Title: \"Weather Alerts\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `alerts-page`: Div, main container\n  - `alerts-list`: Div, displays active alerts\n  - `severity-filter`: Dropdown, filter alerts by severity\n  - `location-filter-alerts`: Dropdown, filter alerts by location\n  - `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}`: Button to acknowledge each alert\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - Filter dropdowns trigger POST requests to update alert list\n  - Buttons `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}` POST to acknowledge alert\n- Context variables:\n  - `alerts` (list of dicts with alert_id, location_id, alert_type, severity, description, start_time, end_time, is_acknowledged)\n  - `severity_filter` (str)\n  - `location_filter` (int or None)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Loop through `alerts` to display details\n  - Buttons have dynamic IDs with alert_id\n\n### 6. templates/air_quality.html\n- Page Title: \"Air Quality Index\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `air-quality-page`: Div, main container\n  - `aqi-display`: Div, display AQI value\n  - `aqi-description`: Div, display AQI description\n  - `pollution-details`: Table, show PM2.5, PM10, NO2, O3\n  - `location-aqi-filter`: Dropdown, filter by location\n  - `health-recommendation`: Div, health recommendations\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - Changing `location-aqi-filter` triggers POST to update AQI data\n- Context variables:\n  - `locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)\n  - `selected_location_id` (int)\n  - `aqi_data` (dict with aqi_index, pm25, pm10, no2, o3, last_updated)\n  - `description` (str)\n  - `health_recommendation` (str)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Show air quality details for selected location\n\n### 7. templates/saved_locations.html\n- Page Title: \"Saved Locations\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `saved-locations-page`: Div, main container\n  - `locations-table`: Table listing saved locations\n  - `view-location-weather-{location_id}`: Button to view weather per location\n  - `remove-location-button-{location_id}`: Button to remove saved location\n  - `add-new-location-button`: Button to add location\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - Buttons with dynamic IDs send POST requests for viewing/removing\n  - `add-new-location-button` navigates to `/search` via `url_for('location_search')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `saved_locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name, temperature, condition)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Loop to generate dynamic buttons with location_id\n\n### 8. templates/settings.html\n- Page Title: \"Settings\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `settings-page`: Div, main container\n  - `temperature-unit-select`: Dropdown for temp units\n  - `default-location-select`: Dropdown for default location\n  - `alert-notifications-toggle`: Checkbox toggle for alerts\n  - `save-settings-button`: Button to save settings\n  - `back-to-dashboard`: Button to navigate back\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - `back-to-dashboard` → `url_for('dashboard')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `temperature_units` (list of str)\n  - `selected_unit` (str)\n  - `saved_locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)\n  - `default_location_id` (int)\n  - `alert_notifications_enabled` (bool)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Render dropdowns and toggles with current saved settings\n  - Provide save button to persist changes\n\n---\n\n## Section 3: Data File Schemas\n\n### 1. Current Weather Data\n- File Path: `data/current_weather.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `location_id` (int): Unique identifier for location\n  2. `location_name` (str): Name of the location\n  3. `temperature` (int or float): Current temperature\n  4. `condition` (str): Weather condition (e.g., Sunny, Rainy)\n  5. `humidity` (int): Humidity percentage\n  6. `wind_speed` (int or float): Wind speed\n  7. `last_updated` (str): Timestamp in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM` format\n- Data Description: Stores current weather conditions for multiple locations\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30\n  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30\n  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30\n  ```\n\n### 2. Forecasts Data\n- File Path: `data/forecasts.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `forecast_id` (int): Unique forecast entry identifier\n  2. `location_id` (int): Identifier of location\n  3. `date` (str): Date of forecast `YYYY-MM-DD`\n  4. `high_temp` (int or float): Forecast high temperature\n  5. `low_temp` (int or float): Forecast low temperature\n  6. `condition` (str): Weather condition\n  7. `precipitation` (int): Percentage chance of precipitation\n  8. `humidity` (int): Forecast humidity percentage\n- Data Description: Daily weather forecast data for locations\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60\n  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70\n  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85\n  ```\n\n### 3. Locations Data\n- File Path: `data/locations.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `location_id` (int): Unique identifier for location\n  2. `location_name` (str): Name of location\n  3. `latitude` (float): Latitude coordinate\n  4. `longitude` (float): Longitude coordinate\n  5. `country` (str): Country name\n  6. `timezone` (str): Time zone abbreviation\n- Data Description: Stores metadata for all known locations\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST\n  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT\n  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST\n  ```\n\n### 4. Weather Alerts Data\n- File Path: `data/alerts.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `alert_id` (int): Unique alert identifier\n  2. `location_id` (int): Location associated with alert\n  3. `alert_type` (str): Type of alert (e.g., Thunderstorm)\n  4. `severity` (str): Alert severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low)\n  5. `description` (str): Alert text description\n  6. `start_time` (str): Start timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`\n  7. `end_time` (str): End timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`\n  8. `is_acknowledged` (int): 0 for false, 1 for true\n- Data Description: Active weather alerts\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0\n  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0\n  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1\n  ```\n\n### 5. Air Quality Data\n- File Path: `data/air_quality.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `aqi_id` (int): Unique air quality entry identifier\n  2. `location_id` (int): Location identifier\n  3. `aqi_index` (int): Air Quality Index value (0-500)\n  4. `pm25` (float): PM2.5 concentration\n  5. `pm10` (float): PM10 concentration\n  6. `no2` (float): NO2 concentration\n  7. `o3` (float): O3 concentration\n  8. `last_updated` (str): Timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`\n- Data Description: Air quality measurements for locations\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30\n  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30\n  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30\n  ```\n\n### 6. Saved Locations Data\n- File Path: `data/saved_locations.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `saved_id` (int): Unique saved location identifier\n  2. `user_id` (int): User identifier (no authentication, can be static or default)\n  3. `location_id` (int): Location identifier\n  4. `location_name` (str): Name of saved location\n  5. `is_default` (int): 1 if location is default, else 0\n- Data Description: Locations saved by users for quick access\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|1|New York|1\n  2|1|2|London|0\n  3|2|3|Tokyo|1\n  ```\n\n",

`view-forecast-button`: Button, navigates to Weekly Forecast page\n  - `view-alerts-button`: Button, navigates to Weather Alerts page\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - `search-location-button` → `url_for('location_search')`\n  - `view-forecast-button` → `url_for('weekly_forecast')`\n  - `view-alerts-button` → `url_for('weather_alerts')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `default_location` (dict) with `location_id`, `location_name`, `temperature`, `condition`\n  - `saved_locations` (list of dicts) with `location_id`, `location_name`\n- Usage notes:\n  - Display current weather summary for `default_location` in `current-weather-summary`\n  - Provide quick navigation buttons to other pages\n\n### 2. templates/current_weather.html\n- Page Title: \"Current Weather\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `location_name` (str)\n  - `temperature` (float/int)\n  - `condition` (str)\n  - `humidity` (int)\n  - `wind_speed` (float/int)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Display detailed weather info for selected location\n\n### 3. templates/weekly_forecast.html\n- Page Title: \"Weekly Forecast\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `back-to-dashboard`: Button, navigates back to dashboard\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - `back-to-dashboard` → `url_for('dashboard')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)\n  - `selected_location_id` (int)\n  - `forecasts` (list of dicts with date, high_temp, low_temp, condition)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Use dropdown `location-filter` to select location and reload forecasts\n  - Loop through `forecasts` to populate `forecast-list` and `forecast-table`\n\n### 4. templates/location_search.html\n- Page Title: \"Search Locations\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `search-page`: Div, main container\n  - `location-search-input`: Input field for search query\n  - `search-results`: Div, displays list of matching locations\n  - `select-location-button-{location_id}`: Button for each search result to select location\n  - `saved-locations-list`: Div, displays previously saved locations\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - Buttons `select-location-button-{location_id}` trigger backend POST to select location\n- Context variables:\n  - `search_results` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name, latitude, longitude)\n  - `saved_locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Dynamic generation of `select-location-button-{location_id}` for each search result\n  - Display saved locations with quick access\n\n### 5. templates/alerts.html\n- Page Title: \"Weather Alerts\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `alerts-page`: Div, main container\n  - `alerts-list`: Div, displays active alerts\n  - `severity-filter`: Dropdown, filter alerts by severity\n  - `location-filter-alerts`: Dropdown, filter alerts by location\n  - `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}`: Button to acknowledge each alert\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - Filter dropdowns trigger POST requests to update alert list\n  - Buttons `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}` POST to acknowledge alert\n- Context variables:\n  - `alerts` (list of dicts with alert_id, location_id, alert_type, severity, description, start_time, end_time, is_acknowledged)\n  - `severity_filter` (str)\n  - `location_filter` (int or None)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Loop through `alerts` to display details\n  - Buttons have dynamic IDs with alert_id\n\n### 6. templates/air_quality.html\n- Page Title: \"Air Quality Index\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `air-quality-page`: Div, main container\n  - `aqi-display`: Div, display AQI value\n  - `aqi-description`: Div, display AQI description\n  - `pollution-details`: Table, show PM2.5, PM10, NO2, O3\n  - `location-aqi-filter`: Dropdown, filter by location\n  - `health-recommendation`: Div, health recommendations\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - Changing `location-aqi-filter` triggers POST to update AQI data\n- Context variables:\n  - `locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)\n  - `selected_location_id` (int)\n  - `aqi_data` (dict with aqi_index, pm25, pm10, no2, o3, last_updated)\n  - `description` (str)\n  - `health_recommendation` (str)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Show air quality details for selected location\n\n### 7. templates/saved_locations.html\n- Page Title: \"Saved Locations\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `saved-locations-page`: Div, main container\n  - `locations-table`: Table listing saved locations\n  - `view-location-weather-{location_id}`: Button to view weather per location\n  - `remove-location-button-{location_id}`: Button to remove saved location\n  - `add-new-location-button`: Button to add location\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - Buttons with dynamic IDs send POST requests for viewing/removing\n  - `add-new-location-button` navigates to `/search` via `url_for('location_search')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `saved_locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name, temperature, condition)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Loop to generate dynamic buttons with location_id\n\n### 8. templates/settings.html\n- Page Title: \"Settings\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `settings-page`: Div, main container\n  - `temperature-unit-select`: Dropdown for temp units\n  - `default-location-select`: Dropdown for default location\n  - `alert-notifications-toggle`: Checkbox toggle for alerts\n  - `save-settings-button`: Button to save settings\n  - `back-to-dashboard`: Button to navigate back\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - `back-to-dashboard` → `url_for('dashboard')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `temperature_units` (list of str)\n  - `selected_unit` (str)\n  - `saved_locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)\n  - `default_location_id` (int)\n  - `alert_notifications_enabled` (bool)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Render dropdowns and toggles with current saved settings\n  - Provide save button to persist changes\n\n---\n\n## Section 3: Data File Schemas\n\n### 1. Current Weather Data\n- File Path: `data/current_weather.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `location_id` (int): Unique identifier for location\n  2. `location_name` (str): Name of the location\n  3. `temperature` (int or float): Current temperature\n  4. `condition` (str): Weather condition (e.g., Sunny, Rainy)\n  5. `humidity` (int): Humidity percentage\n  6. `wind_speed` (int or float): Wind speed\n  7. `last_updated` (str): Timestamp in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM` format\n- Data Description: Stores current weather conditions for multiple locations\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30\n  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30\n  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30\n  ```\n\n### 2. Forecasts Data\n- File Path: `data/forecasts.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `forecast_id` (int): Unique forecast entry identifier\n  2. `location_id` (int): Identifier of location\n  3. `date` (str): Date of forecast `YYYY-MM-DD`\n  4. `high_temp` (int or float): Forecast high temperature\n  5. `low_temp` (int or float): Forecast low temperature\n  6. `condition` (str): Weather condition\n  7. `precipitation` (int): Percentage chance of precipitation\n  8. `humidity` (int): Forecast humidity percentage\n- Data Description: Daily weather forecast data for locations\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60\n  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70\n  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85\n  ```\n\n### 3. Locations Data\n- File Path: `data/locations.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `location_id` (int): Unique identifier for location\n  2. `location_name` (str): Name of location\n  3. `latitude` (float): Latitude coordinate\n  4. `longitude` (float): Longitude coordinate\n  5. `country` (str): Country name\n  6. `timezone` (str): Time zone abbreviation\n- Data Description: Stores metadata for all known locations\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST\n  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT\n  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST\n  ```\n\n### 4. Weather Alerts Data\n- File Path: `data/alerts.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `alert_id` (int): Unique alert identifier\n  2. `location_id` (int): Location associated with alert\n  3. `alert_type` (str): Type of alert (e.g., Thunderstorm)\n  4. `severity` (str): Alert severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low)\n  5. `description` (str): Alert text description\n  6. `start_time` (str): Start timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`\n  7. `end_time` (str): End timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`\n  8. `is_acknowledged` (int): 0 for false, 1 for true\n- Data Description: Active weather alerts\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0\n  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0\n  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1\n  ```\n\n### 5. Air Quality Data\n- File Path: `data/air_quality.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `aqi_id` (int): Unique air quality entry identifier\n  2. `location_id` (int): Location identifier\n  3. `aqi_index` (int): Air Quality Index value (0-500)\n  4. `pm25` (float): PM2.5 concentration\n  5. `pm10` (float): PM10 concentration\n  6. `no2` (float): NO2 concentration\n  7. `o3` (float): O3 concentration\n  8. `last_updated` (str): Timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`\n- Data Description: Air quality measurements for locations\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30\n  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30\n  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30\n  ```\n\n### 6. Saved Locations Data\n- File Path: `data/saved_locations.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `saved_id` (int): Unique saved location identifier\n  2. `user_id` (int): User identifier (no authentication, can be static or default)\n  3. `location_id` (int): Location identifier\n  4. `location_name` (str): Name of saved location\n  5. `is_default` (int): 1 if location is default, else 0\n- Data Description: Locations saved by users for quick access\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|1|New York|1\n  2|1|2|London|0\n  3|2|3|Tokyo|1\n  ```\n\n",

`selected_location_id` (int)\n  - `forecasts` (list of dicts with date, high_temp, low_temp, condition)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Use dropdown `location-filter` to select location and reload forecasts\n  - `saved-locations-list`: Div, displays previously saved locations\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - Buttons `select-location-button-{location_id}` trigger backend POST to select location\n- Context variables:\n  - `search_results` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name, latitude, longitude)\n  - `saved_locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Dynamic generation of `select-location-button-{location_id}` for each search result\n  - `severity_filter` (str)\n  - `location_filter` (int or None)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Loop through `alerts` to display details\n  - Buttons have dynamic IDs with alert_id\n\n### 6. templates/air_quality.html\n- Page Title: \"Air Quality Index\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)\n  - `selected_location_id` (int)\n  - `aqi_data` (dict with aqi_index, pm25, pm10, no2, o3, last_updated)\n  - `description` (str)\n  - `health_recommendation` (str)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Buttons with dynamic IDs send POST requests for viewing/removing\n  - `add-new-location-button` navigates to `/search` via `url_for('location_search')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `saved_locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name, temperature, condition)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Loop to generate dynamic buttons with location_id\n\n### 8. templates/settings.html\n- Page Title: \"Settings\"\n- Element IDs:\n  - `settings-page`: Div, main container\n  - `temperature-unit-select`: Dropdown for temp units\n  - `default-location-select`: Dropdown for default location\n  - `alert-notifications-toggle`: Checkbox toggle for alerts\n  - `save-settings-button`: Button to save settings\n  - `back-to-dashboard`: Button to navigate back\n- Navigation mappings:\n  - `back-to-dashboard` → `url_for('dashboard')`\n- Context variables:\n  - `temperature_units` (list of str)\n  - `selected_unit` (str)\n  - `saved_locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)\n  - `default_location_id` (int)\n  - `alert_notifications_enabled` (bool)\n- Usage notes:\n  - Render dropdowns and toggles with current saved settings\n  - Provide save button to persist changes\n\n---\n\n## Section 3: Data File Schemas\n\n### 1. Current Weather Data\n- File Path: `data/current_weather.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `location_id` (int): Unique identifier for location\n  2. `location_name` (str): Name of the location\n  3. `temperature` (int or float): Current temperature\n  4. `condition` (str): Weather condition (e.g., Sunny, Rainy)\n  5. `humidity` (int): Humidity percentage\n  6. `wind_speed` (int or float): Wind speed\n  7. `last_updated` (str): Timestamp in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM` format\n- Data Description: Stores current weather conditions for multiple locations\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30\n  2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30\n  3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30\n  ```\n\n### 2. Forecasts Data\n- File Path: `data/forecasts.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `forecast_id` (int): Unique forecast entry identifier\n  2. `location_id` (int): Identifier of location\n  3. `date` (str): Date of forecast `YYYY-MM-DD`\n  4. `high_temp` (int or float): Forecast high temperature\n  5. `low_temp` (int or float): Forecast low temperature\n  6. `condition` (str): Weather condition\n  7. `precipitation` (int): Percentage chance of precipitation\n  8. `humidity` (int): Forecast humidity percentage\n- Data Description: Daily weather forecast data for locations\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60\n  2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70\n  3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85\n  ```\n\n### 3. Locations Data\n- File Path: `data/locations.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `location_id` (int): Unique identifier for location\n  2. `location_name` (str): Name of location\n  3. `latitude` (float): Latitude coordinate\n  4. `longitude` (float): Longitude coordinate\n  5. `country` (str): Country name\n  6. `timezone` (str): Time zone abbreviation\n- Data Description: Stores metadata for all known locations\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST\n  2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT\n  3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST\n  ```\n\n### 4. Weather Alerts Data\n- File Path: `data/alerts.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `alert_id` (int): Unique alert identifier\n  2. `location_id` (int): Location associated with alert\n  3. `alert_type` (str): Type of alert (e.g., Thunderstorm)\n  4. `severity` (str): Alert severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low)\n  5. `description` (str): Alert text description\n  6. `start_time` (str): Start timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`\n  7. `end_time` (str): End timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`\n  8. `is_acknowledged` (int): 0 for false, 1 for true\n- Data Description: Active weather alerts\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0\n  2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0\n  3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1\n  ```\n\n### 5. Air Quality Data\n- File Path: `data/air_quality.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `aqi_id` (int): Unique air quality entry identifier\n  2. `location_id` (int): Location identifier\n  3. `aqi_index` (int): Air Quality Index value (0-500)\n  4. `pm25` (float): PM2.5 concentration\n  5. `pm10` (float): PM10 concentration\n  6. `no2` (float): NO2 concentration\n  7. `o3` (float): O3 concentration\n  8. `last_updated` (str): Timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`\n- Data Description: Air quality measurements for locations\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30\n  2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30\n  3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30\n  ```\n\n### 6. Saved Locations Data\n- File Path: `data/saved_locations.txt`\n- Format: Pipe-delimited\n- Fields (in order):\n  1. `saved_id` (int): Unique saved location identifier\n  2. `user_id` (int): User identifier (no authentication, can be static or default)\n  3. `location_id` (int): Location identifier\n  4. `location_name` (str): Name of saved location\n  5. `is_default` (int): 1 if location is default, else 0\n- Data Description: Locations saved by users for quick access\n- Example Lines:\n  ```\n  1|1|1|New York|1\n  2|1|2|London|0\n  3|2|3|Tokyo|1\n  ```\n\n",

a moderate health concern for a very small number of people.\")\n    elif aqi_index <= 150:\n        return (\"Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups\", \"Members of sensitive groups may experience health (\"Hazardous\", \"Health warnings of emergency conditions. The entire population is more likely to be affected.\")\n\n\n# Route 1: Root Redirect\n@app.route('/')\ndef root_redirect():\n    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n\n# Route 2: Dashboard Page\n@app.route('/dashboard')\ndef dashboard():\n    saved_locs = load_saved_locations()\n    current_weather = load_current_weather()\n    # Determine default_location\n    default_location = None\n    for saved in saved_locs:\n        if saved['is_default']:\n            default_location = saved\n            break\n    if default_location:\n        # enrich default_location with temperature and condition\n        for cw in current_weather:\n            if cw['location_id'] == default_location['location_id']:\n                default_location_weather = {\n                    'location_id': cw['location_id'],\n                    'location_name': cw['location_name'],\n                    'temperature': cw['temperature'],\n                    'condition': cw['condition']\n                }\n                break\n        else:\n            # no current weather found for default location\n            default_location_weather = {\n                'location_id': default_location['location_id'],\n                'location_name': default_location['location_name'],\n                'temperature': None,\n                'condition': ''\n            }\n    else:\n        # If no default location, just pick first saved if exists\n        if saved_locs:\n            first = saved_locs[0]\n            default_location_weather = {\n                'location_id': first['location_id'],\n                'location_name': first['location_name'],\n                'temperature': None,\n                'condition': ''\n            }\n        else:\n            # No saved locations at all\n            default_location_weather = {\n                'location_id': 0,\n                'location_name': '',\n                'temperature': None,\n                'condition': ''\n            }\n    # Prepare saved_locations list for context\n    saved_locations_summary = []\n    for loc in saved_locs:\n        saved_locations_summary.append({\n            'location_id': loc['location_id'],\n            'location_name': loc['location_name']\n        })\n    return render_template('dashboard.html',\n                           default_location=default_location_weather,\n                           saved_locations=saved_locations_summary)\n\n\n# Route 3: Current Weather Page\n@app.route('/weather/current/<int:location_id>')\ndef current_weather(location_id):\n    current_weather_data = load_current_weather()\n    for cw in current_weather_data:\n        if cw['location_id'] == location_id:\n            return render_template('current_weather.html',\n                                   location_name=cw['location_name'],\n                                   temperature=cw['temperature'],\n                                   condition=cw['condition'],\n                                   humidity=cw['humidity'],\n                                   wind_speed=cw['wind_speed'])\n    # Location not found, render with empty/default values\n    return render_template('current_weather.html',\n                           location_name='',\n                           temperature=None,\n                           condition='',\n                           humidity=0,\n                           wind_speed=0)\n\n\n# Route 4: Weekly Forecast Page\n@app.route('/forecast/weekly', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef weekly_forecast():\n    locations = load_locations()\n    forecasts_all = load_forecasts()\n    selected_location_id = None\n\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        selected_location_id = request.form.get('location_filter')\n        if selected_location_id is not None:\n            try:\n                selected_location_id = int(selected_location_id)\n            except ValueError:\n                selected_location_id = None\n\n    if selected_location_id is None and locations:\n        selected_location_id = locations[0]['location_id']\n\n    # Filter forecasts for selected location\n    forecasts = []\n    if selected_location_id is not None:\n        for forecast in forecasts_all:\n            if forecast['location_id'] == selected_location_id:\n                forecasts.append({\n                    'date': forecast['date'],\n                    'high_temp': forecast['high_temp'],\n                    'low_temp': forecast['low_temp'],\n                    'condition': forecast['condition']\n                })\n\n    return render_template('weekly_forecast.html',\n                           locations=[{'location_id': loc['location_id'], 'location_name': loc['location_name']} for loc in locations],\n                           selected_location_id=selected_location_id,\n                           forecasts=forecasts)\n\n\n# Route 5: Location Search Page\n@app.route('/search', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef location_search():\n    locations = load_locations()\n    saved_locs = load_saved_locations()\n    saved_locations = [{'location_id': loc['location_id'], 'location_name': loc['location_name']} for loc in saved_locs]\n    search_results = []\n\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        # Search query from form\n        query = request.form.get('search_query', '').strip().lower()\n        if query:\n            search_results = [\n                {\n                    'location_id': loc['location_id'],\n                    'location_name': loc['location_name'],\n                    'latitude': loc['latitude'],\n                    'longitude': loc['longitude']\n                }\n                for loc in locations if query in loc['location_name'].lower()\n            ]\n        # Check if user selected a location to save\n        selected_location_id = request.form.get('select_location_id')\n        if selected_location_id:\n            try:\n                selected_location_id = int(selected_location_id)\n                # In specification, no persistence needed, only display\n                # So no file write or modify here\n                pass\n            except ValueError:\n                pass\n\n    return render_template('location_search.html',\n                           search_results=search_results,\n                           saved_locations=saved_locations)\n\n\n# Route 6: Weather Alerts Page\n@app.route('/alerts', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef weather_alerts():\n    alerts = load_alerts()\n    saved_locs = load_saved_locations()\n    severity_filter = ''  # Default no filter\n    location_filter = None\n\n    # Get filter values from POST or GET\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        severity_filter = request.form.get('severity_filter', '')\n        loc_filter_val = request.form.get('location_filter')\n        try:\n            location_filter = int(loc_filter_val) if loc_filter_val else None\n        except ValueError:\n            location_filter = None\n        # Check if acknowledgment button pressed\n        ack_alert_id = request.form.get('acknowledge_alert_id')\n        if ack_alert_id:\n            # Acknowledgment is non-persistent as no db or file write specified\n            # We simulate acknowledgment by marking in the list temporarily\n            try:\n                ack_alert_id = int(ack_alert_id)\n                for alert in alerts:\n                    if alert['alert_id'] == ack_alert_id:\n                        alert['is_acknowledged'] = True\n                        break\n            except ValueError:\n                pass\n\n    # Apply filters\n    filtered_alerts = []\n    for alert in alerts:\n        if severity_filter and alert['severity'] != severity_filter:\n            continue\n        if location_filter is not None and alert['location_id'] != location_filter:\n            continue\n        filtered_alerts.append(alert)\n\n    return render_template('alerts.html',\n                           alerts=filtered_alerts,\n                           severity_filter=severity_filter,\n                           location_filter=location_filter)\n\n\n# Route 7: Air Quality Page\n@app.route('/air_quality', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef air_quality():\n    locations = load_locations()\n    air_quality_data = load_air_quality()\n    selected_location_id = None\n\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        loc_id = request.form.get('location_aqi_filter')\n        try:\n            selected_location_id = int(loc_id) if loc_id else None\n        except ValueError:\n            selected_location_id = None\n\n    if selected_location_id is None and locations:\n        selected_location_id = locations[0]['location_id']\n\n    aqi_data = {\n        'aqi_index': 0,\n        'pm25': 0.0,\n        'pm10': 0.0,\n        'no2': 0.0,\n        'o3': 0.0,\n        'last_updated': ''\n    }\n    description = ''\n    health_recommendation = ''\n\n    for aqi in air_quality_data:\n        if aqi['location_id'] == selected_location_id:\n            aqi_data = {\n                'aqi_index': aqi['aqi_index'],\n                'pm25': aqi['pm25'],\n                'pm10': aqi['pm10'],\n                'no2': aqi['no2'],\n                'o3': aqi['o3'],\n                'last_updated': aqi['last_updated']\n            }\n            description, health_recommendation = get_aqi_description_and_recommendation(aqi_data['aqi_index'])\n            break\n\n    return render_template('air_quality.html',\n                           locations=[{'location_id': loc['location_id'], 'location_name': loc['location_name']} for loc in locations],\n                           selected_location_id=selected_location_id,\n                           aqi_data=aqi_data,\n                           description=description,\n                           health_recommendation=health_recommendation)\n\n\n# Route 8: Saved Locations Page\n@app.route('/saved_locations', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef saved_locations():\n    saved_locs = load_saved_locations()\n    current_weather = load_current_weather()\n\n    # POST handling for view/remove not specified for persistence (no file write), so just ignore actual changes\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        # We could have keys to view or remove locations\n        # But no persistence required per spec\n        pass\n\n    saved_locations_list = []\n    for saved in saved_locs:\n        # find current weather for location\n        temp = None\n        cond = ''\n        for cw in current_weather:\n            if cw['location_id'] == saved['location_id']:\n                temp = cw['temperature']\n                cond = cw['condition']\n                break\n        saved_locations_list.append({\n            'location_id': saved['location_id'],\n            'location_name': saved['location_name'],\n            'temperature': temp,\n            'condition': cond\n        })\n\n    return render_template('saved_locations.html',\n                           saved_locations=saved_locations_list)\n\n\n# Route 9: Settings Page\n@app.route('/settings', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef settings():\n    saved_locs = load_saved_locations()\n    temperature_units = [\"Celsius\", \"Fahrenheit\", \"Kelvin\"]\n    # Defaults\n    selected_unit = \"Celsius\"\n    default_location_id = 0\n    alert_notifications_enabled = False\n\n    # Load current settings from a pseudo persistence file (not specified in spec, so defaults only)\n    # POST: update settings (not persisted to disk as no file specified)\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        selected_unit = request.form.get('temperature_unit', selected_unit)\n        try:\n request.form.get('location_filter')\n        try:\n            location_filter = int(loc_filter_val) if loc_filter_val else None\n        except ValueError:\n            location_filter = None\n        # Check if acknowledgment button pressed\n        ack_alert_id = request.form.get('acknowledge_alert_id')\n        if ack_alert_id:\n            # Acknowledgment is non-persistent as no db or file write specified\n            # We simulate acknowledgment by marking in the list temporarily\n            try:\n                ack_alert_id = int(ack_alert_id)\n                for alert in alerts:\n                    if alert['alert_id'] == ack_alert_id:\n                        alert['is_acknowledged'] = True\n                        break\n            except ValueError:\n                pass\n\n    # Apply filters\n    filtered_alerts = []\n    for alert in alerts:\n        if severity_filter and alert['severity'] != severity_filter:\n            continue\n        if location_filter is not None and alert['location_id'] != location_filter:\n            continue\n        filtered_alerts.append(alert)\n\n    return render_template('alerts.html',\n                           alerts=filtered_alerts,\n                           locations=locations,\n                           selected_location_id=location_filter,\n                           severity_filter=severity_filter,\n                           location_filter=location_filter)\n\n\n# Route 7: Air Quality Page\n@app.route('/air_quality', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef request.form.get('location_aqi_filter')\n        try:\n            selected_location_id = int(loc_id) if loc_id else None\n        except ValueError:\n            selected_location_id = None\n\n    if selected_location_id is None and locations:\n        selected_location_id = locations[0]['location_id']\n\n    aqi_data = {\n        'aqi_index': 0,\n        'pm25': 0.0,\n        'pm10': 0.0,\n        'no2': 0.0,\n        'o3': 0.0,\n        'last_updated': ''\n    }\n    description = ''\n    health_recommendation = ''\n\n    for aqi in air_quality_data:\n        if aqi['location_id'] == selected_location_id:\n            aqi_data = {\n                'aqi_index': aqi['aqi_index'],\n                'pm25': aqi['pm25'],\n                'pm10': aqi['pm10'],\n                'no2': aqi['no2'],\n                'o3': aqi['o3'],\n                'last_updated': aqi['last_updated']\n            }\n            description, health_recommendation = get_aqi_description_and_recommendation(aqi_data['aqi_index'])\n            break\n\n    return render_template('air_quality.html',\n                           locations=[{'location_id': loc['location_id'], 'location_name': loc['location_name']} for loc in locations],\n                           selected_location_id=selected_location_id,\n                           aqi_data=aqi_data,\n                           description=description,\n                           health_recommendation=health_recommendation)\n\n\n# Route 8: Saved Locations Page\n@app.route('/saved_locations', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef saved_locations():\n    saved_locs = load_saved_locations()\n    current_weather = load_current_weather()\n\n    # POST handling for view/remove not specified for persistence (no file write), so just ignore actual changes\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        # We could have keys to view or remove locations\n        # But no persistence required per spec\n        pass\n\n    saved_locations_list = []\n    for saved in saved_locs:\n        # find current weather for location\n        temp = None\n        cond = ''\n        for cw in current_weather:\n            if cw['location_id'] == saved['location_id']:\n                temp = cw['temperature']\n                cond = cw['condition']\n                break\n        saved_locations_list.append({\n            'location_id': saved['location_id'],\n            'location_name': saved['location_name'],\n            'temperature': temp,\n            'condition': cond\n        })\n\n    return render_template('saved_locations.html',\n                           saved_locations=saved_locations_list)\n\n\n# Route 9: Settings Page\n@app.route('/settings', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef settings():\n    saved_locs = load_saved_locations()\n    temperature_units = [\"Celsius\", \"Fahrenheit\", \"Kelvin\"]\n    # Defaults\n    selected_unit = \"Celsius\"\n    default_location_id = 0\n    alert_notifications_enabled = False\n\n    # Load current settings from a pseudo persistence file (not specified in spec, so defaults only)\n    # POST: update settings (not persisted to disk as no file specified)\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        selected_unit = request.form.get('temperature_unit', selected_unit)\n        try:\n            default_location_id = int(request.form.get('default_location_id', default_location_id))\n        except ValueError:\n            default_location_id = 0\n        alert_notifications_enabled = (\"Good\", \"Air quality is satisfactory and poses little or no risk.\")\n    elif aqi_index <= 100:\n        return (\"Moderate\", \"Air quality is acceptable; however, some pollutants may be a moderate health concern for a very small number of people.\")\n    elif aqi_index <= 150:\n        return (\"Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups\", \"Members of sensitive groups may experience health effects; general public is less likely to be affected.\")\n    elif aqi_index <= 200:\n        return (\"Unhealthy\", \"Everyone may begin to experience health effects; members of sensitive groups may experience more serious effects.\")\n    elif aqi_index <= 300:\n        return (\"Very Unhealthy\", \"Health alert: everyone may experience more serious health effects.\")\n    else:\n        return (\"Hazardous\", \"Health warnings of emergency conditions. The entire population is more likely to be affected.\")\n\n\n# Route 1: Root Redirect\n@app.route('/')\ndef root_redirect():\n    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n\n# Route 2: Dashboard Page\n@app.route('/dashboard')\ndef dashboard():\n    saved_locs = load_saved_locations()\n    current_weather = load_current_weather()\n    # Determine default_location\n    default_location = None\n    for saved in saved_locs:\n        if saved['is_default']:\n            default_location = saved\n            break\n    if default_location:\n        # enrich default_location with temperature and        # If no default location, just pick first saved if exists\n        if saved_locs:\n            first = saved_locs[0]\n            default_location_weather = {\n                'location_id': first['location_id'],\n                'location_name': first['location_name'],\n                'temperature': None,\n                'condition': ''\n            }\n        else:\n            # No saved locations at all\n            default_location_weather = {\n                'location_id': 0,\n                'location_name': '',\n                'temperature': None,\n 4: Weekly Forecast Page\n@app.route('/forecast/weekly', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef weekly_forecast():\n    locations = load_locations()\n    forecasts_all = load_forecasts()\n    selected_location_id = None\n\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        selected_location_id = request.form.get('location_filter')\n        if selected_location_id is not None:\n            try:\n                selected_location_id = int(selected_location_id)\n            except ValueError:\n                selected_location_id = None\n\n    # If GET with location_id query param\n    if              selected_location_id = None\n\n    if selected_location_id is None and locations:\n        selected_location_id = locations[0]['location_id']\n\n    # Filter forecasts for selected location\n    forecasts = []\n    if selected_location_id is not None:\n        for forecast in forecasts_all:\n            if forecast['location_id'] == selected_location_id:\n 'condition': forecast['condition']\n                })\n\n    return render_template('weekly_forecast.html',\n                           locations=[{'location_id': loc['location_id'], 'location_name': pass\n\n    # Apply filters\n    filtered_alerts = []\n    for alert in alerts:\n        if severity_filter and alert['severity'] != severity_filter:\n            continue\n        if location_filter (\"Good\", \"Air quality is satisfactory and poses little or no risk.\")\n    elif aqi_index <= 100:\n        return (\"Moderate\", \"Air quality is acceptable; however, some pollutants may be a moderate health concern for a very small number of people.\")\n    elif aqi_index <= 150:\n        return (\"Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups\", \"Members of sensitive groups may experience health saved_locations=saved_locations)\n\n\n# Route 6: Weather Alerts Page\n@app.route('/alerts', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef weather_alerts():\n    alerts = load_alerts()\n    saved_locs = load_saved_locations()\n    locations = [{'location_id': loc['location_id'], 'location_name': loc['location_name']} for loc in saved_locs]\n    severity_filter = ''  # Default no filter\n    location_filter = None\n\n    # Get filter values from POST or GET\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        severity_filter = request.form.get('severity_filter', '')\n        loc_filter_val = request.form.get('location_filter')\n        try:\n            location_filter = int(loc_filter_val) if loc_filter_val else None\n        except ValueError:\n            location_filter = None\n        # Check if acknowledgment button pressed\n        ack_alert_id = request.form.get('acknowledge_alert_id')\n        if ack_alert_id:\n            # Acknowledgment is non-persistent as no db or file write specified\n            # We simulate acknowledgment by marking in the list temporarily\n            try:\n                ack_alert_id = int(ack_alert_id)\n                for alert in alerts:\n                    if alert['alert_id'] == ack_alert_id:\n                        alert['is_acknowledged'] = True\n                        break\n            except ValueError:\n                pass\n\n    # Apply filters\n    filtered_alerts = []\n    for alert in alerts:\n        if severity_filter and alert['severity'] != severity_filter:\n            continue\n        if location_filter is not None and alert['location_id'] != location_filter:\n            continue\n        filtered_alerts.append(alert)\n\n    return render_template('alerts.html',\n                           alerts=filtered_alerts,\n                           locations=locations,\n                           selected_location_id=location_filter,\n                           severity_filter=severity_filter,\n                           location_filter=location_filter)\n\n\n# Route 7: Air Quality Page\n@app.route('/air_quality', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef air_quality():\n    locations = load_locations()\n    air_quality_data = load_air_quality()\n    selected_location_id = None\n\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        loc_id = request.form.get('location_aqi_filter')\n        try:\n            selected_location_id = int(loc_id) if loc_id else None\n        except ValueError:\n            selected_location_id = None\n\n    if selected_location_id is None and locations:\n        selected_location_id = locations[0]['location_id']\n\n    aqi_data = {\n        'aqi_index': 0,\n        'pm25': 0.0,\n        'pm10': 0.0,\n        'no2': 0.0,\n        'o3': 0.0,\n        'last_updated': ''\n    }\n    description = ''\n    health_recommendation = ''\n\n    for aqi in air_quality_data:\n        if aqi['location_id'] == selected_location_id:\n            aqi_data = {\n                'aqi_index': aqi['aqi_index'],\n                'pm25': aqi['pm25'],\n                'pm10': aqi['pm10'],\n                'no2': aqi['no2'],\n                'o3': aqi['o3'],\n                'last_updated': aqi['last_updated']\n            }\n            description, health_recommendation = get_aqi_description_and_recommendation(aqi_data['aqi_index'])\n            break\n\n    return render_template('air_quality.html',\n                           locations=[{'location_id': loc['location_id'], 'location_name': loc['location_name']} for loc in locations],\n                           selected_location_id=selected_location_id,\n                           aqi_data=aqi_data,\n                           description=description,\n                           health_recommendation=health_recommendation)\n\n\n# Route 8: Saved Locations Page\n@app.route('/saved_locations', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef saved_locations():\n    saved_locs = load_saved_locations()\n    current_weather = load_current_weather()\n\n    # POST handling for view/remove not specified for persistence (no file write), so just ignore actual changes\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        # We could have keys to view or remove locations\n        # But no persistence required per spec\n        pass\n\n    saved_locations_list = []\n    for saved in saved_locs:\n        # find current weather for location\n        temp = None\n        cond = ''\n        for cw in current_weather:\n            if cw['location_id'] == saved['location_id']:\n                temp = cw['temperature']\n                cond = cw['condition']\n                break\n        saved_locations_list.append({\n            'location_id': saved['location_id'],\n            'location_name': saved['location_name'],\n            'temperature': temp,\n            'condition': cond\n        })\n\n    return render_template('saved_locations.html',\n                           saved_locations=saved_locations_list)\n\n\n# Route 9: Settings Page\n@app.route('/settings', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef settings():\n    saved_locs = load_saved_locations()\n    temperature_units = [\"Celsius\", \"Fahrenheit\", \"Kelvin\"]\n    # Defaults\n    selected_unit = \"Celsius\"\n    default_location_id = 0\n    alert_notifications_enabled = False\n\n    # Load current settings from a pseudo persistence file (not specified in spec, so defaults only)\n    # POST: update settings (not persisted to disk as no file specified)\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        selected_unit = request.form.get('temperature_unit', selected_unit)\n        try:\n            default_location_id = int(request.form.get('default_location_id', default_location_id))\n        except ValueError:\n            default_location_id = 0\n        alert_notifications_enabled =          === design_spec.md ===
         # Design Specification Document for WeatherForecast Web Application
         
         ---
         
         ## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification
         
         ### 1. Root Route
         - URL Path: `/`
         - Methods Allowed: `GET`
         - Function Name: `root_redirect`
         - Behavior: Redirects to `/dashboard` route
         - Template: None
         - Context Variables: None
         
         ### 2. Dashboard Page
         - URL Path: `/dashboard`
         - Methods Allowed: `GET`
         - Function Name: `dashboard`
         - Template Filename: `dashboard.html`
         - Context Variables:
           - `default_location`: `dict` with fields:
             - `location_id` (int)
             - `location_name` (str)
             - `temperature` (float or int)
             - `condition` (str)
           - `saved_locations`: list of dicts with fields:
             - `location_id` (int)
             - `location_name` (str)
         
         ### 3. Current Weather Page
         - URL Path: `/weather/current/<int:location_id>`
         - Methods Allowed: `GET`
         - Function Name: `current_weather`
         - Template Filename: `current_weather.html`
         - Context Variables:
           - `location_name`: `str`
           - `temperature`: `float` or `int`
           - `condition`: `str`
           - `humidity`: `int` (percentage)
           - `wind_speed`: `float` or `int`
         
         ### 4. Weekly Forecast Page
         - URL Path: `/forecast/weekly`
         - Methods Allowed: `GET`, `POST`
         - Function Name: `weekly_forecast`
         - Template Filename: `weekly_forecast.html`
         - Context Variables:
           - `locations`: list of dicts with fields:
             - `location_id` (int)
             - `location_name` (str)
           - `selected_location_id`: `int`
           - `forecasts`: list of dicts with fields:
             - `date` (str, formatted as `YYYY-MM-DD`)
             - `high_temp` (float or int)
             - `low_temp` (float or int)
             - `condition` (str)
         
         ### 5. Location Search Page
         - URL Path: `/search`
         - Methods Allowed: `GET`, `POST`
         - Function Name: `location_search`
         - Template Filename: `location_search.html`
         - Context Variables:
           - `search_results`: list of dicts with fields:
             - `location_id` (int)
             - `location_name` (str)
             - `latitude` (float)
             - `longitude` (float)
           - `saved_locations`: list of dicts with fields:
             - `location_id` (int)
             - `location_name` (str)
         
         ### 6. Weather Alerts Page
         - URL Path: `/alerts`
         - Methods Allowed: `GET`, `POST`
         - Function Name: `weather_alerts`
         - Template Filename: `alerts.html`
         - Context Variables:
           - `alerts`: list of dicts with fields:
             - `alert_id` (int)
             - `location_id` (int)
             - `alert_type` (str)
             - `severity` (str)
             - `description` (str)
             - `start_time` (str, formatted `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`)
             - `end_time` (str, formatted `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`)
             - `is_acknowledged` (bool)
           - `severity_filter`: `str`
           - `location_filter`: `int` or `None`
         
         ### 7. Air Quality Page
         - URL Path: `/air_quality`
         - Methods Allowed: `GET`, `POST`
         - Function Name: `air_quality`
         - Template Filename: `air_quality.html`
         - Context Variables:
           - `locations`: list of dicts with fields:
             - `location_id` (int)
             - `location_name` (str)
           - `selected_location_id`: `int`
           - `aqi_data`: `dict` with fields:
             - `aqi_index` (int)
             - `pm25` (float)
             - `pm10` (float)
             - `no2` (float)
             - `o3` (float)
             - `last_updated` (str, formatted `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`)
           - `description`: `str`
           - `health_recommendation`: `str`
         
         ### 8. Saved Locations Page
         - URL Path: `/saved_locations`
         - Methods Allowed: `GET`, `POST`
         - Function Name: `saved_locations`
         - Template Filename: `saved_locations.html`
         - Context Variables:
           - `saved_locations`: list of dicts with fields:
             - `location_id` (int)
             - `location_name` (str)
             - `temperature` (float or int)
             - `condition` (str)
         
         ### 9. Settings Page
         - URL Path: `/settings`
         - Methods Allowed: `GET`, `POST`
         - Function Name: `settings`
         - Template Filename: `settings.html`
         - Context Variables:
           - `temperature_units`: list of `str` (e.g., ["Celsius", "Fahrenheit", "Kelvin"])
           - `selected_unit`: `str`
           - `saved_locations`: list of dicts with fields:
             - `location_id` (int)
             - `location_name` (str)
           - `default_location_id`: `int`
           - `alert_notifications_enabled`: `bool`
         
         ---
         
         ## Section 2: Frontend HTML Templates Specification
         
         ### 1. templates/dashboard.html
         - Page Title: "Weather Dashboard"
         - Element IDs:
           - `dashboard-page`: Div, main container for dashboard
           - `current-weather-summary`: Div, summary of current default location weather
           - `search-location-button`: Button, navigates to Location Search page
           - `view-forecast-button`: Button, navigates to Weekly Forecast page
           - `view-alerts-button`: Button, navigates to Weather Alerts page
         - Navigation mappings:
           - `search-location-button` → `url_for('location_search')`
           - `view-forecast-button` → `url_for('weekly_forecast')`
           - `view-alerts-button` → `url_for('weather_alerts')`
         - Context variables:
           - `default_location` (dict) with `location_id`, `location_name`, `temperature`, `condition`
           - `saved_locations` (list of dicts) with `location_id`, `location_name`
         - Usage notes:
           - Display current weather summary for `default_location` in `current-weather-summary`
           - Provide quick navigation buttons to other pages
         
         ### 2. templates/current_weather.html
         - Page Title: "Current Weather"
         - Element IDs:
           - `current-weather-page`: Div, main container
           - `location-name`: H1, displays location name
           - `temperature-display`: Div, displays temperature
           - `weather-condition`: Div, displays current weather condition
           - `humidity-info`: Div, humidity percentage
           - `wind-speed-info`: Div, wind speed
         - Navigation mappings:
           - Provide 'Back to Dashboard' link/button using `url_for('dashboard')`
         - Context variables:
           - `location_name` (str)
           - `temperature` (float/int)
           - `condition` (str)
           - `humidity` (int)
           - `wind_speed` (float/int)
         - Usage notes:
           - Display detailed weather info for selected location
         
         ### 3. templates/weekly_forecast.html
         - Page Title: "Weekly Forecast"
         - Element IDs:
           - `forecast-page`: Div, main container
           - `forecast-table`: Table, displays daily forecasts (date, high temp, low temp, condition)
           - `location-filter`: Dropdown, filter forecasts by location
           - `forecast-list`: Div, grid display of forecast cards for each day
           - `back-to-dashboard`: Button, navigates back to dashboard
         - Navigation mappings:
           - `back-to-dashboard` → `url_for('dashboard')`
         - Context variables:
           - `locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)
           - `selected_location_id` (int)
           - `forecasts` (list of dicts with date, high_temp, low_temp, condition)
         - Usage notes:
           - Use dropdown `location-filter` to select location and reload forecasts
           - Loop through `forecasts` to populate `forecast-list` and `forecast-table`
         
         ### 4. templates/location_search.html
         - Page Title: "Search Locations"
         - Element IDs:
           - `search-page`: Div, main container
           - `location-search-input`: Input field for search query
           - `search-results`: Div, displays list of matching locations
           - `select-location-button-{location_id}`: Button for each search result to select location
           - `saved-locations-list`: Div, displays previously saved locations
         - Navigation mappings:
           - Buttons `select-location-button-{location_id}` trigger backend POST to select location
         - Context variables:
           - `search_results` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name, latitude, longitude)
           - `saved_locations` (list of dicts with location_id, location_name)

           - `severity-filter`: Dropdown, filter alerts by severity
           - `location-filter-alerts`: Dropdown, filter alerts by location
           - `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}`: Button to acknowledge each alert
         - Navigation mappings:
           - Filter dropdowns trigger POST requests to update alert list
           - Buttons `acknowledge-alert-button-{alert_id}` POST to acknowledge alert
         - Context variables:
           - `alerts` (list of dicts with alert_id, location_id, alert_type, severity, description, start_time, end_time, is_acknowledged)
           - `severity_filter` (str)
           - `location_filter` (int or None)

         ### 6. templates/air_quality.html

         ---
         
         ## Section 3: Data File Schemas
         
         ### 1. Current Weather Data
         - File Path: `data/current_weather.txt`
         - Format: Pipe-delimited
         - Fields (in order):
           1. `location_id` (int): Unique identifier for location
           2. `location_name` (str): Name of the location
           3. `temperature` (int or float): Current temperature
           4. `condition` (str): Weather condition (e.g., Sunny, Rainy)
           5. `humidity` (int): Humidity percentage
           6. `wind_speed` (int or float): Wind speed
           7. `last_updated` (str): Timestamp in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM` format
         - Data Description: Stores current weather conditions for multiple locations
         - Example Lines:
           ```
           1|New York|72|Sunny|65|10|2025-01-20 14:30
           2|London|55|Rainy|80|15|2025-01-20 14:30
           3|Tokyo|45|Cloudy|72|8|2025-01-20 14:30
           ```
         
         ### 2. Forecasts Data
         - File Path: `data/forecasts.txt`
         - Format: Pipe-delimited
         - Fields (in order):
           1. `forecast_id` (int): Unique forecast entry identifier
           2. `location_id` (int): Identifier of location
           3. `date` (str): Date of forecast `YYYY-MM-DD`
           4. `high_temp` (int or float): Forecast high temperature
           5. `low_temp` (int or float): Forecast low temperature
           6. `condition` (str): Weather condition
           7. `precipitation` (int): Percentage chance of precipitation
           8. `humidity` (int): Forecast humidity percentage
         - Data Description: Daily weather forecast data for locations
         - Example Lines:
           ```
           1|1|2025-01-21|75|60|Sunny|0|60
           2|1|2025-01-22|68|55|Cloudy|10|70
           3|2|2025-01-21|58|48|Rainy|80|85
           ```
         
         ### 3. Locations Data
         - File Path: `data/locations.txt`
         - Format: Pipe-delimited
         - Fields (in order):
           1. `location_id` (int): Unique identifier for location
           2. `location_name` (str): Name of location
           3. `latitude` (float): Latitude coordinate
           4. `longitude` (float): Longitude coordinate
           5. `country` (str): Country name
           6. `timezone` (str): Time zone abbreviation
         - Data Description: Stores metadata for all known locations
         - Example Lines:
           ```
           1|New York|40.7128|-74.0060|USA|EST
           2|London|51.5074|-0.1278|UK|GMT
           3|Tokyo|35.6762|139.6503|Japan|JST
           ```
         
         ### 4. Weather Alerts Data
         - File Path: `data/alerts.txt`
         - Format: Pipe-delimited
         - Fields (in order):
           1. `alert_id` (int): Unique alert identifier
           2. `location_id` (int): Location associated with alert
           3. `alert_type` (str): Type of alert (e.g., Thunderstorm)
           4. `severity` (str): Alert severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low)
           5. `description` (str): Alert text description
           6. `start_time` (str): Start timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`
           7. `end_time` (str): End timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`
           8. `is_acknowledged` (int): 0 for false, 1 for true
         - Data Description: Active weather alerts
         - Example Lines:
           ```
           1|1|Thunderstorm|High|Severe thunderstorm warning in effect until 8 PM|2025-01-20 14:00|2025-01-20 20:00|0
           2|2|Fog|Medium|Dense fog advisory in effect until noon tomorrow|2025-01-20 06:00|2025-01-21 12:00|0
           3|1|Wind|Medium|High wind advisory with gusts up to 45 mph|2025-01-20 15:00|2025-01-21 09:00|1
           ```
         
         ### 5. Air Quality Data
         - File Path: `data/air_quality.txt`
         - Format: Pipe-delimited
         - Fields (in order):
           1. `aqi_id` (int): Unique air quality entry identifier
           2. `location_id` (int): Location identifier
           3. `aqi_index` (int): Air Quality Index value (0-500)
           4. `pm25` (float): PM2.5 concentration
           5. `pm10` (float): PM10 concentration
           6. `no2` (float): NO2 concentration
           7. `o3` (float): O3 concentration
           8. `last_updated` (str): Timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`
         - Data Description: Air quality measurements for locations
         - Example Lines:
           ```
           1|1|45|12.5|35|28|55|2025-01-20 14:30
           2|2|67|22.3|48|42|78|2025-01-20 14:30
           3|3|120|68.5|95|65|110|2025-01-20 14:30
           ```
         
         ### 6. Saved Locations Data
         - File Path: `data/saved_locations.txt`
         - Format: Pipe-delimited
         - Fields (in order):
           1. `saved_id` (int): Unique saved location identifier
           2. `user_id` (int): User identifier (no authentication, can be static or default)
           3. `location_id` (int): Location identifier
           4. `location_name` (str): Name of saved location
           5. `is_default` (int): 1 if location is default, else 0
         - Data Description: Locations saved by users for quick access
         - Example Lines:
           ```
           1|1|1|New York|1
           2|1|2|London|0
           3|2|3|Tokyo|1
           ```
